key_insight: Molten-salt etching of Ti2InB2 with ZnCl2 directly converts it into multilayer Ti2B2Clx MBene with chlorine surface terminations, while theory explains why this pathway is favorable.
engineering_relevance: This offers a practical route to synthesize new 2D boride MBenes for energy-storage and other advanced materials applications.
method: Selective molten-salt etching of Ti2InB2 using ZnCl2
STEM/EDS/EELS to verify In removal and Cl termination
In situ XRD plus DFT to confirm direct transformation and thermodynamic favorability
evidence_signal: Experiments showed complete removal of In and Cl coverage of about 1.1–1.4, with in situ XRD indicating a direct biphasic transition and initial Li-ion tests showing stable capacity.
watch_item: Scalability and long-term electrochemical stability still need validation, especially because termination coverage and phase purity can affect reproducibility.
paper_title: Synthesis of Ti2B2Clx MBenes in molten salts from theoretical and experimental perspectives
